mechanism_narrative: A low federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, increasing the present value of future corporate earnings and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS is below 1% over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 6.0% signals labor market deterioration that forces downward revisions to corporate earnings expectations, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the following 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE exceeds 6.0%, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the mean return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in the consumer price index and the federal funds rate jointly raise real discount rates and compress profit margins, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS both increase month-over-month
failure_condition: In periods where CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS rise simultaneously, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive more than 45% of the time, or the interaction term in a linear model has a t-statistic with absolute value below 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in retail sales forecasts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days when the unemployment rate is below 5.5%, as strong demand converts into revenue surprises.
failure_condition: When UNRATE is below 5.5%, month-over-month RSAFS growth fails to predict positive 5-day S&P 500 returns with Rank IC > 0.05 over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerations in industrial production signal expanding supply-side capacity and rising manufacturing earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month INDPRO increases are followed by negative S&P 500 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in housing starts exceeding 5 percent over three months foreshadows contraction in construction activity and related consumption, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: After HOUST declines more than 5% over 3 months, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered events.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in payroll employment and increases in the unemployment rate confirm a broad labor market downturn that reduces aggregate income, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS decreases month-over-month while UNRATE increases
failure_condition: When PAYEMS declines month-over-month and UNRATE rises simultaneously, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of joint occurrences, or the combined signal's Rank IC is below 0.04 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly increases in real GDP reflect aggregate demand strength that translates into positive earnings revisions, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarter-over-quarter GDPC1 increases are followed by negative S&P 500 60-day returns in more than 50% of observations over a 5-year rolling window.
